prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Action from MM".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Locate the webhook node named "Slash Cmd from MM".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the scheduler node "Every hour".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "publish report" node (n8n-nodes-base.mattermost) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "get user data" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "open-standup-dialog?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "config?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "open config dialog" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prep Config Dialog" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "callback ID?" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "standup-config" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "standup-answers" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prep Config Override" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Override Config" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Read Config 1" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Read Config 2" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "confirm success" node (n8n-nodes-base.mattermost) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Read Config 3" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter Due Standups" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prep Request Standup" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Channel" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Remind Users" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get User" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prep Reminder" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prep Standup Dialog" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "open standup dialog" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prep Report" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Delete ReminderPost" node (n8n-nodes-base.mattermost) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Post" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
